agents.lastMessage: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position

report.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 and TM5, while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity and reducing off-target affinity.

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is necessary but insufficient. The next iteration must prioritize disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to achieve improved selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to mediate off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions allows for targeted disruption of conserved pocket contacts.
Introducing mutations at these positions that are less likely to form conserved hydrogen bonds or hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 will reduce off-target binding.
